source.title: Limbo - Edward Caplette - Joel Ainsworth (Toreador)
source.localPath: source-sheets\Characters in Limbo\Limbo - Edward Caplette - Joel Ainsworth (Toreador).xlsx

rows:
Ever since I was little, I loved to draw. I would doodle on everything. Honestly though, I was never good at it. The picture in my mind never really translated well through my hands and onto paper. I did, however, have a knack for computers and programming. Naturally my parents and teachers encouraged me to put down the sketchbook and take up the keyboard instead. I can’t really say it was a bad call. I excelled tremendously and had recruiters beating down my door even before I finished college. Still, I never lost my passion for drawing, so I decided to put my programming skills to work, and create software that could help anyone become the artist they dream to be. “Digital Artist” revolutionized the art industry. One piece of software could replace thousands of dollars in art supplies and equipment. The digital artist was born. 

Several updates and editions followed, making me a wealthy man. I was considered the founding father of a new era in art. The old guard hated me for it. I didn’t set out to start a revolution, but I was at the center of one now.

The president of my distribution partner introduced me to Ilene Bennett; a beautiful business woman who donated heavily to the arts and was a well known patron of up and coming artists. We shared a passion for change, progress and shared a mutual respect for one another. I invited her over for a drink and our conversation carried on into the night. That night she offered me immortality and an opportunity to breathe new life into the “Clan of the Rose”. 

What can I say? She was very convincing, and I love a good challenge. 

The scorn and resistance I received from the mortal art world was nothing compared to the old fossils of Clan Toreador. At least those old men had wardrobes that belonged to this century! Though they don’t understand what I’ve created, nor do they respect my talent; they fear me because I am the writing on the wall. The world is changing. Art is changing. They can’t seem to change themselves.
#1 Janet Wadsworth (Malkavian) she is a wonderful DJ, an asset you could try and claim for you and your clan, and she is in LA
#2 Samuel Baird (Nosferatu) he is supposed to be some whiz with computers, he may be a useful ally who at least understands your artwork with the code.
#3 Paul Cotte (Toreador) you have heard about him, he is the old guard.  The type that used to be an ARTIST, he’s nothing but washed up  now.
#4 George Kershaw ( Toreador Primogen, Seattle WA, out of game) George said to call if you need anything, he would be willing to see what he could do.

rows: #1. Promote and patron the art of the digital age. Spread your vision,even if it means going against the old guard.
#2. Partner with other Kindred or secure venture capital for a new business endeavor that promotes your artistic philosophy.
#3. Build a safe haven for young like-minded Kindred.
Some fucks tried to burn down your haven
